title: Championship Trivia
description: A challenging final round across history, science, sports, geography, and culture.

questions.prompt: Which scientist proposed the three laws of motion in 'Principia Mathematica'?
questions.hint: The same scientist is associated with universal gravitation.
questions.explanation: Isaac Newton presented the three laws of motion in 'Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica.' For further study, review classical mechanics.

questions.hint: The site is high in the Andes of Peru.
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca civilization. For further study, explore Andean history and Inca engineering.

questions.prompt: The Suez Canal connects the Mediterranean Sea and the Red Sea.
questions.hint: It is a major shipping route through Egypt.
questions.explanation: The Suez Canal links the Mediterranean Sea with the Red Sea. For further study, review global trade routes and canal history.

questions.prompt: The process by which liquid water becomes vapor is _____.
questions.hint: It is part of the water cycle.
questions.explanation: Evaporation is the process of liquid water becoming water vapor. For further study, review phase changes and the water cycle.

questions.prompt: Which composer wrote the four-concerto set known as 'The Four Seasons'?
questions.hint: He was an Italian Baroque composer.
questions.explanation: Antonio Vivaldi composed 'The Four Seasons.' For further study, review Baroque music and programmatic composition.

questions.prompt: Which country won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
questions.hint: The host nation won the tournament.
questions.explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930. For further study, review early World Cup history.

questions.prompt: Which author created the detective Sherlock Holmes?
questions.hint: The stories often feature Dr. Watson.
questions.explanation: Arthur Conan Doyle created Sherlock Holmes. For further study, review the development of detective fiction.

questions.prompt: The element carbon is found in diamonds and graphite.
questions.hint: They are different forms of the same element.
questions.explanation: Diamonds and graphite are allotropes of carbon. For further study, review crystal structures and allotropes.

questions.prompt: Which treaty ended the American Revolutionary War?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Paris of 1783 ended the American Revolutionary War. For further study, review the diplomacy of the American Revolution.

questions.prompt: Which island is shared by Haiti and the Dominican Republic?
questions.hint: It is the second-largest island in the Caribbean.
questions.explanation: Hispaniola is shared by Haiti and the Dominican Republic. For further study, review Caribbean geography.

questions.prompt: The ancient trade route network linking East and West is known as the Silk _____.
questions.hint: Its name refers to a valuable textile.
questions.explanation: The Silk Road connected regions of Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and Europe through trade. For further study, explore cross-cultural exchange along trade routes.

questions.prompt: Which telescope, launched in 1990, transformed public images of deep space?
questions.hint: It shares a name with astronomer Edwin Hubble.
questions.explanation: The Hubble Space Telescope launched in 1990 and produced many influential space images. For further study, review space telescope missions.
